description: Per-agent layer for Claude Code. Maps each tool name + input to an ontology action and a target field, declares the harness-internal markers (session-title, suggestion-mode) and structural roles (thinking, tool_use), and names agent-owned well-known paths. Command→action interpretation is NOT here — it is project/tech-specific and lives in project grounding's `commands`. Every `action` and `object` value here MUST be an id defined in ontology/coding.json.

targetKinds.path: Take the named input field as a filesystem path; hand it to project grounding to resolve an object type and label.
targetKinds.host: Reduce a URL field to its host for display.
targetKinds.literal: Use the field value verbatim as the display target.

wellKnownPaths.note: Paths the AGENT owns, wherever they live on disk (not project-relative). First regex match wins; the label is the full semantic name and suppresses the grounded-type suffix. This is Claude Code's own config layout, so it belongs here, not in project grounding.

NotebookEdit.note: edit_mode disambiguates the semantic action deterministically from the input — no model needed.

SlashCommand.note: A user/project-defined command whose effect is arbitrary (it expands to a prompt), so it is `run`, not `use-skill`. Field is `command` (e.g. '/review-pr 123').

Bash.note: Freeform shell escape hatch — labeled by tool name only. `commentField` surfaces the agent's own 5-10 word `description` verbatim as the node's `comment` (display only, never part of `what`).

_unknownTool.note: Any tool not listed (including mcp__* and project slash commands) falls back to: action `act`, object `unknown`, phrase = lowercased tool name. Surfaced as low-confidence so the gap is visible.

markers.note: Deterministic short-circuits at the llm_request level — when a rule matches, it fully determines the label and pre-empts any model call.

structuralRoles.note: Deterministic prefix phrases derived from the shape of the response (a thinking block, a trailing tool call). Prepended to — not replacing — any model classification of a final message.

modelResidual.note: What is LEFT for the weak model after all of the above. Only fires for a genuine terminal assistant message (a final message that does not end in a tool call). The model classifies the ACT into an ordered list of phrases, each a verb from the ontology's `messageActs` paired with a generic object (e.g. ['confirm edit','suggest next steps']); it never quotes the message. Everything else is deterministic. messageActs is a distinct axis from the leaf `actions` (which are derived, not modeled); the model prompt is generated from that list, so add a verb to the ontology rather than editing any prompt.
